name: JupyterHub REST API
what_it_does: Multi-user server for Jupyter notebooks with REST API for managing users, spawning notebook servers, and administering the hub — enabling data science teams to share computational environments at scale with central authentication and resource management.
use_cases: Programmatically spawning and managing user notebook servers via REST API for automated workflows
Administering JupyterHub users, groups, and server lifecycles for data platform automation
Integrating JupyterHub with LDAP, OAuth, or custom authenticators via API-driven configuration
Auto-scaling notebook server pools based on demand using the admin API
Agent-driven notebook execution by creating servers, running kernels, and collecting outputs
not_for: Individual Jupyter notebook use without multi-user management (use Jupyter Lab directly)
Hosted notebook platforms without self-hosting capability (use Google Colab or Deepnote)
Production ML model serving (use MLflow or Seldon)
best_when: A data platform or agent needs to programmatically manage multi-user Jupyter notebook servers, spawn environments, and administer user access in a self-hosted deployment.
avoid_when: You just need to run notebooks — self-hosting JupyterHub has significant operational overhead. Use a managed notebook platform for simplicity.

auth.notes: Admin API tokens or user tokens. OAuth2 for user authentication (GitHub, Google, LDAP via plugins). Token-based API access with scope controls. Admin tokens have full access; user tokens scoped to their resources.

pricing.paid_tiers: 2i2c and other vendors offer managed JupyterHub hosting; AWS/GCP have managed variants
pricing.notes: BSD 3-Clause open source. Jupyter project is under NumFOCUS. Free to self-host but requires significant infrastructure (Kubernetes recommended for scale). JupyterHub The Big Notebook (TLJH) for single-server deployments.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; limited by server resources

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers users, groups, servers, and services. Token-scoped access. Well-documented API with OpenAPI spec. MCP server exists for notebook interaction. Server spawn operations are async — must poll for readiness.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation idempotent (409 if exists). Server spawn idempotent if already running. Token creation always creates new tokens. Group membership idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Server spawn is async — must poll /users/{name}/server until server is ready
Kernel execution requires Jupyter Server API (separate from JupyterHub admin API)
Admin token required for user management — user tokens only access own resources
Spawner configuration (resource limits, images) is server-side — not configurable via API at spawn time without custom spawner

security_score_components.security_notes: BSD 3-Clause open source — auditable. OAuth2 support for enterprise auth. Token scopes limit blast radius. TLS configurable (not enforced by default). No formal certifications. Self-hosted — operator responsible for security hardening. CWEs exist in history — keep updated.
